error: CheckpointLoaderSimple: Model in folder 'checkpoints' with filename 'SDXL/sd_xl_base_1.0.safetensors' not found.

note: Signature strength — neon night market, wet reflective pavement, strong vanishing-point depth; distant figures atmospheric so no anatomy risk. Dark and embeddable, perfect on a dark doc page.

Golden-hour valley with river bend, mist, mountains; clean geometry, leading-line composition. Only ding: warm bright sky/ground is a hard-baked light field that fights a dark doc — needs cropping/subjecting.

Team huddled around a laptop reads correctly; bodies and faces coherent (no fused face or extra limb visible, so no anatomy cap). Hands around the laptop are small and mushy — the typical SD1.5 finger softness — hence the artifact dock, not a hard ≤2 cap.

Green upward bar/line trend reads as a chart, but axis ticks and labels are gibberish smear — no legible numbers, painterly bars instead of crisp vector. Illegible-text signal: route this category to the vector handlers, not this base.

Round cream blob with one large eye — clearly a mascot, clean and centered with a clear focal point. Adher docked: single-eye/cyclops design is a touch generic rather than a distinctive character. Bright cream ground fights a dark doc but is cleanly subjectable as a sprite.

base_trait: SD1.5 versatile all-rounder — exceptional cinematic/atmospheric scenes and landscapes, clean simple mascots, competent office stock (small hands mush at scale); cannot render legible chart text — line-goes-up axis labels come out as gibberish glyphs, so that category must be routed to vector handlers.

note: Neon night-market street, wet reflective pavement, crowd of silhouetted figures receding into depth — unmistakably the asked neon-market scene. Figures are small backlit silhouettes so no anatomy exposed; faux signage glyphs are illegible but purely atmospheric (not a text-intent category). Naturally dark, drops straight onto a dark doc page.

River valley winding through forested hills under a dramatic storm with visible rain shafts and warm rim-light. Photoreal, clean geometry, strong focal river leading the eye. Minor: bright sky band is a touch luminous for a dark page but easily embeddable/subjectable.

Three women collaborating around a laptop — team-at-a-laptop brief delivered, faces coherent, no extra limbs or melted features (no anatomy cap triggered). Deductions: keyboard hands are partially obscured (mild artifact risk), a hard-baked Apple logo on the lid is a brand artifact, and the bright sunlit window background fights a dark doc unless cropped.

Green area chart that does trend upward (peaks then dips), so the rising-line intent is legible — but EVERY label is gibberish: garbled title glyphs, smeared axis numbers, a nonsense '6 + 70' subtitle, and two legend boxes of pseudo-text. Classic SD1.5 illegible-text failure (artifact capped). Bright white dashboard card also fights a dark page. Route this category to the vector handlers, not this model.

Rounded yellow helmeted robot-blob with big sparkly eyes and a soft smile — clearly a mascot, clean cartoon linework, coherent symmetry, drop shadow. Minor artifact: two stray floating orange pill shapes flanking the head (disconnected elements). Light ground fights a dark doc but a mascot is trivially cut out / subjected.

base_trait: SD1.5 photoreal — excellent on neon scenes, photoreal landscapes, and clean cartoon mascots; renders coherent multi-person office stock with usable (if partly obscured) anatomy, but like all SD1.5 it bakes gibberish glyphs into any chart/label, so line-goes-up must be routed to the vector handlers.

note: neon night-market street reads instantly; strong leading-line depth, moody dark palette sits natively on a dark page. Background signage is faux-glyph gibberish but that is decorative scene-dressing, not a label, so no cap. Walking figures are silhouetted/blurred — no exposed anatomy to break.

river valley with forested hills, dramatic clouds, golden light — clean geometry, no artifacts, S-curve river leads the eye. Best cell. Sky a touch bright but tonally muted enough to subject onto dark doc.

team of five around a conference table with laptops reads correctly. Faces are soft/waxy and hands-on-keyboards are mushy and untrustworthy (classic SD1.5 hand failure) — artifact docked to 2, but no flagrant extra/fused limb so the hard anatomy cap is not triggered. Window glare is bright; subjectable. Inspect hands before shipping.

reads as a green bar-chart dashboard and the top-right sparkline does tick up, but ALL text is gibberish: title is 'Gowfh growh', x-axis labels scramble to 'Annual Sa / Auar Augus', y-values and legend nums are nonsense ('307', '9.057', '390 5'). Bars/gridlines are coherent but the data is illegible. Gibberish-text signal — route this category to a vector handler, not this base.

two charming flat-vector robot mascots, big eyes, clean coherent forms, no melt (left mascot's single cyclops eye is intentional design, not a defect). Balanced pair. BUT hard-baked bright lavender background fights a dark doc page — needs background removal/subjecting before embed; docfit docked to 2.

base_trait: SD1.5 photoreal — landscapes and neon night-scenes are excellent and dark-page native; cute-mascot is clean but ships a bright background that must be subjected; cannot render legible chart text (line-goes-up is gibberish — route to vector) and human hands in office crowds are soft/unreliable, so people-central shots need inspection.

note: neon night-market street, wet reflective pavement, dense glowing signage, silhouetted crowd to a clean central vanishing point; atmospheric sign-glyphs (not a labeled-text category, no penalty); dark-mode native
forested river valley with storm light breaking through cloud; coherent geometry, no artifacts, river as leading line; naturally moody/dark, sits cleanly on a dark page

five people around a table with two laptops; faces coherent, hands plausible at res, no extra-limb/melt disqualifier; slightly generic stock framing and bright back windows but mid-tone and embeddable

renders a dashboard with green bars that DESCEND left-to-right (no upward line); every label is gibberish glyphs ('Gan Netris','Maxbell','SISDOME', garbled axis text). Classic SD1.5 chart-text failure — route to a vector handler, not this base

single eyeball-bodied character, yellow rounded base, big blue eye, blush, halo ring; clean 3d-cartoon vector look. Minor artifact: odd red slash marks on both foot nubs. White ground fights a dark doc but is flat and cleanly subjectable

base_trait: SD1.5 3d-cartoon checkpoint — exceptional at atmospheric neon scenes, moody landscapes, and clean vector mascots, and surprisingly clean on multi-person office stock (no mangled anatomy); but like all SD1.5 it bakes gibberish glyphs and cannot render legible chart/UI text, and here it even inverts the brief (descending bars, no upward line) — never route line-goes-up to it.

note: Neon rainy night-market street with silhouetted crowd, vivid wet reflections and strong receding depth — unmistakably the asked scene. Signage carries faux-glyph atmospheric 'text' (illegible but decorative, not the requested content), the only artifact. Restrained dark palette sits perfectly on a dark doc page.

Golden-hour river valley winding through forested hills under dramatic clouds. Clean geometry, no melted forms, painterly and balanced with a clear S-curve focal flow. Bright sky band is a touch luminous against a dark page but cleanly embeddable/subjectable.

Five professionals around a laptop-strewn conference table — exact category and specifics. No hard anatomy violation (faces coherent, hands on laptops not melted), but characteristic SD1.5 soft-focus blurs the central figure's features. Bright window-lit ground fights a dark doc and needs treatment to embed.

Renders a bar chart (not a line) whose bars do trend upward, so 'goes-up' reads loosely. Hard failure: every x-axis label is gibberish glyph-soup, the centered caption ('NUB 3:1'-like) is garbled, and the legend dots are meaningless — illegible-text signal. Must NOT be routed this category; vector handlers own it.

One-eyed yellow blob mascot with clean dark-outline vector linework, a single coherent iris, antenna with highlight, two stubby feet — adorable and on-brand. No deformed forms. Light grey ground is bright but cleanly subjectable for a dark page.

base_trait: SD1.5 animated/illustrative checkpoint — exceptional at painterly neon scenes, atmospheric landscapes, and clean stylized mascots; renders believable office-stock with characteristic soft faces; cannot produce legible chart axis/label text — bakes gibberish glyphs, so line-goes-up must be routed to the vector handlers.

note: Neon night-market street, wet reflective ground, strong atmospheric depth and figure silhouettes; signage is illegible scribble but scenes need no legible text. Naturally dark — ideal dark-mode fit.
River valley with forested slopes, mountains and golden cloud light; coherent painterly geometry, leading-line river composition, no artifacts. Best cell on the sheet.

Five professionals around a table with two laptops, windows behind — clear category and prompt hit. Faces coherent, no extra limbs; hands mostly cropped/hidden (SD1.5 dodge), standing man's tie/far-arm area is slightly muddy. No anatomy cap triggered.

Category + prompt miss: bars trend DOWNWARD left-to-right (tallest left, shortest right) — opposite of 'line goes up'. Every label is gibberish glyphs ('Gan Netris','MasSell','Adlustdoer', scrambled axis ticks/legend). Bright white card fights a dark doc. Route to vector handlers.

Round one-eyed yellow creature, clean vector-style shading, appealing and clearly a mascot. Artifact: the two lower feet/hands carry red gash-like marks that read as wounds/cuts. Light-grey ground, easily subjectable.

base_trait: SD1.5 artistic — excellent atmospheric scenes and painterly landscapes, solid office-stock with coherent faces (hands hidden); but bakes gibberish glyphs and even inverts the trend on chart/UI categories, so it cannot render legible line-goes-up — route that category to vector handlers.

note: neon cyberpunk corridor with deep one-point perspective, wet reflections, clean magenta/cyan signage; native-dark, embeds on a dark page with zero rework
aerial forested valley with winding river, ridgelines and atmospheric haze; coherent geometry, no artifacts; mildly bright/warm for a dark doc but easily embeddable

team-around-a-laptop stock shot; faces and clustered hands read coherent at sheet res with no third arm/fused face, but hands in tight groups are the residual risk; bright background fights a dark page

clear green upward-trending line on dark grid — concept lands and is dark-native, but axis/tick labels are illegible gibberish glyphs; usable as decorative concept art, NOT as a real chart — route actual charts to a vector handler
rounded friendly robot, symmetrical, big eyes, clean form; rendered/photoreal rather than flat vector and on a light studio ground, so needs subjecting for a dark page but cuts out cleanly

base_trait: SDXL photoreal (Juggernaut) — exceptional dark-native scenes and clean landscapes, reliable office-stock anatomy, appealing mascots; renders an upward-trend concept convincingly but bakes illegible gibberish axis text, so real charts must go to a vector handler.

note: Neon rainy night street/market — deep one-point perspective, wet reflective pavement, glowing signage. Photoreal, clean, already dark-grounded. Reference-grade scene cell.
Winding road through green rolling hills, soft atmospheric haze. Reads unmistakably as a landscape, clean geometry, good depth. Bright sky band is the only mild dark-page friction; easily embeddable.

Canonical office-stock: team gathered around a laptop in a bright modern room. People central — anatomy held, no extra limbs / melted hands / fused faces visible, so no hard cap. Bright blue-white ground fights a dark doc; needs a panel or treatment to embed.

Abstract green upward glow/line on black — pretty and dark-native, but it is mood, not a chart: no axes, no ticks, no labels. Adherence to an actual 'line chart' is low. No gibberish glyphs baked in (so no text-failure flag), but route real charts to a vector handler, not this raster model.
Round blue-eyed friendly robot, symmetric and clean, clearly a mascot. Rendered semi-photoreal rather than flat illustration — usable, centered, no artifacts. Lighter subject on dark sits fine.

base_trait: SDXL stock-photo checkpoint — nails photoreal scenes, landscapes, and office-team shots with clean anatomy; produces only an abstract glow for line-goes-up (no real axes or labels), so charts must be routed to a vector handler.

note: Neon cyberpunk night market reads instantly — wet reflective ground, layered stall signage, atmospheric depth, walking silhouettes. Background figures slightly soft but no mangled anatomy. Dark palette sits perfectly on a dark doc page.

Golden-hour mountain valley with a misty river, layered ridgelines, pine slopes and dramatic clouds. Clean geometry, strong focal recession to the lit horizon. Bright sky upper third is the only mild dark-mode friction; subjectable.

Three colleagues around a laptop in a glass conference room — clearly the asked subject. Hands read plausibly (woman's hand on keyboard, man's hand by the laptop, no extra limbs). Slightly generic stock vibe; bright daylit ground fights a dark page, needs framing.

GIBBERISH TEXT (cap): convincing dark dashboard chrome but it renders a bar histogram, not the asked upward line; every label is glyph-noise — header reads 'GRENE…', axis ticks and legend are illegible smears. Artifact-freedom capped at 1. Dark ground is doc-friendly but the data is fictional — route to a vector handler.

Adorable chibi robot mascot — clean bold-line cartoon style true to the cartoon-mascot family. Symmetric antennae, big glossy eyes, crisp outlines, no melted limbs. Flat lavender backdrop is hard-baked and bright; trivially keyable but as-is fights a dark doc.

base_trait: SDXL cartoon-mascot model — exceptional at stylized illustration (mascots), neon scenes and painterly landscapes, with clean SDXL anatomy on office stock; but like other raster checkpoints it bakes gibberish glyph-text on chart dashboards and ignores the 'upward line' spec — never route line-goes-up here, the vector handlers own it.

note: neon cyberpunk market with deep atmospheric perspective and glowing signage; clean geometry, strong focal depth, already dark so sits naturally on a dark page. Signage shapes are non-legible neon glow but the category asks for no text, so not a gibberish failure.
green mountain valley with a winding river and layered ridgelines; coherent terrain, good aerial-perspective depth, no artifacts. Bright daylight ground fights a dark doc but is cleanly subjectable.

team gathered around a laptop reads correctly, but the stylized-3d render is glossy/CGI rather than documentary stock; hands on and near the laptop are waxy and indistinct (borderline anatomy, not a flagrant extra-limb). Bright white office ground clashes with a dark doc.

the upward green trend line is clearly present and the dark ground is doc-friendly, but the axis/tick labels are gibberish pseudo-glyphs — the explicit signal this model must NOT be routed chart text; send line-goes-up to a vector handler instead.
glossy blue-and-white robot mascot, big friendly eyes, centered with a clear focal point — the stylized-3d sweet spot. Coherent geometry, appealing, cleanly subjectable onto a dark page.

base_trait: SDXL stylized-3d — glossy CGI rendering excels at mascots and atmospheric neon scenes; landscapes read clean; competent on office stock but renders bright daylight grounds and slightly waxy/indistinct hands that fight a dark doc; bakes gibberish glyphs for chart axis labels, so line-goes-up must route to a vector handler, not this model.

note: Neon cyberpunk market alley — strong atmospheric depth, saturated magenta/cyan signage, clean geometry, naturally dark and dark-doc-native. Reference-grade SDXL photoreal scene.

Aerial winding forest road — coherent terrain, believable serpentine S-curve leading line, natural light, no artifacts. Slightly busy midtone-green ground is less dark-native than scenes but embeds fine.

Team around a central woman; faces read coherent so no anatomy cap, but background figures show indistinct/merged hands and softened secondary faces — typical SDXL crowd-hand ambiguity. Bright office-white ground fights a dark doc page. Usable stock, not flawless.

Glowing green upward line on a dark grid — dark-native and pretty, and the upward line is present (no category miss), but it is decorative glow, not a real chart: no legible axis labels or numbers. Model does not render legible chart text; route actual labelled charts to vector handlers.
Blue-and-white round robot, centered, large friendly eyes, clean symmetric design, strong focal point, no artifacts. Light studio ground needs subjecting for a dark page but cleanly subjectable.

base_trait: SDXL photoreal-3d — exemplary atmospheric scenes, natural landscapes, and clean centered mascots, all dark-doc-friendly; office crowds suffer the usual SDXL background-hand/secondary-face ambiguity, and it renders decorative glowing trend-lines but cannot produce legible chart text (route real charts to vector handlers).

note: cyberpunk neon wet-street with strong depth and reflections; reads unmistakably as a scene. Neon signage is faux-glyph gibberish (acceptable ambiance, not a text category) and distant pedestrians are loosely formed but non-central; excellent dark-doc fit

golden-hour mountain valley with river, conifers and sunburst; clean photoreal SDXL, no artifacts, balanced rule-of-thirds. Luminous bright sky fights a dark doc page somewhat but crops/embeds cleanly

five-person team around two laptops in a glass office — textbook office-stock, faces coherent and hands intact (no extra limbs / melted hands, so no anatomy cap). Slightly busy framing and a bright window background that fights a dark page

dark-native dashboard with a clear green upward-trending area/line chart (category recognisable, no miss-cap). But axis numbers, legend, and the bottom button-row labels are baked gibberish glyphs — illegible text attempt (artifact cap ≤2). Route labels to the vector handler

centered white/blue robot mascot, big expressive eyes, clean symmetry, no anatomy issues (robot, not human; minor claw asymmetry only). Neutral warm-grey ground is trivially subject-able for a dark doc

base_trait: SDXL-Lightning photoreal — nails photoreal landscapes, cyberpunk scenes, office-stock people (coherent faces/hands), and clean centered mascots; renders a dark-native upward chart but bakes gibberish axis/legend/button text, so line-goes-up must be routed to the vector handlers for labels. Lightning trades a little fine fidelity for ~3-4x speed but holds anatomy at this resolution.
